pi_items.source: Web search: Verified that these NORAD IDs correspond to NOAA 15, 18, 19, METOP-B, and METOP-C, which are valid Cospas-Sarsat LEOSAR satellites.

Matches explicit task description instructions ("Latitude = 42.3541 N"). However, OSM reverse geocoding shows this is the Northern Avenue Bridge, ~1.5km from the actual Coast Guard Sector Boston base.
Matches explicit task description instructions ("Longitude = 71.0488 W" -> -71.0488).
Matches explicit task description instructions ("Altitude = 5 meters").
Matches explicit task description instructions ("Latitude = 38.9351 N"). OSM reverse geocoding shows this points to a house in Cape May, not the actual Training Center.
Matches explicit task description instructions ("Longitude = 74.9060 W" -> -74.9060).
Matches explicit task description instructions ("Altitude = 3 meters").
Web search: Cross-referenced each NORAD ID and verified exact matches for each satellite name.

privileged_info_summary: I confirmed that the target satellites (NOAA 15, 18, 19, METOP-B, and METOP-C) are real Cospas-Sarsat LEOSAR satellites, and their specified NORAD IDs (25338, 28654, 33591, 38771, 43689) correctly match real-world registries. I verified that the required coordinates and altitudes for Boston and Cape May exactly match the explicit instructions in the prompt. Additionally, I discovered that these prompt-provided coordinates do not point to the actual Coast Guard facilities (Boston Sector is ~1.5km away; Cape May TRACEN is ~1km away), but the test scripts merely check for approximate latitude/longitude matching the prompt values. Finally, I discovered that the task is defective because "verifier.py" is entirely missing from the environment, rendering the task programmatically unscorable.
